let me just pull up typical slides here.
So a bit about our journey, you know, some of this started where Marcel.
CEO and co-founder was at DeepGram, you know, previously there's a lot of success in building quantum engines at stops like IBM, Service Titan, et cetera.
And just starting to think, hey, you know, while this is amazing, the results you can drive, it's also quite expensive to build these teams.
There's a lot of friction and coordination that needs to happen.

And so at DeepGram, the hypothesis was it's like, as I mentioned, can we start to create these workflows with experts in the loop that allows us to really accelerate how we create content and what are then the results we can drive and what by automating, what does that provide us capacity to do?
And so at DeepGram, starting to scale things like an AI apps catalog, amongst many other tactics that just help them really accelerate growth from the top of funnel perspective on traffic.
But I'm finding a variety of ways of also converting that down funnel.
So they also saw Forex close one deals.

They started to scale this out, and we just started sharing some of this, you know, started doing workshops, sharing a bit of, like, how we built these workflows, the approach, et cetera, and I was one of the folks that joined the first workshop, and while there was a lot of excitement, the reality a lot of folks like myself faced was, like, I have a lot of commitments to existing OKRs and other projects and just capacity constraints that it is going to be a challenge for me to find the capacity these workflows myself or get my team trained up and enabled to build these workflows, and also to think about how we adapt our strategy a bit into bringing this into the mix, and that just started to, you know, there was a clear need in the market and started to, that's where GrowthX really started, was starting to bring this to businesses like a home base, like a Reddit, like a ramp, and early on, you know, we had some conviction that we could develop high quality the content
That would resonate.
But in those early days, it was still not yet proven that you can scale out content that has a heavy AI component to it in its generation.
Is that actually going to produce results?
As we saw these results, you know, that was really validating for us that there's something here that was resonating with a lot of others and just started to double down on the business.
As we thought about, you know, the construction of it were a lot of former marketers who have been in-house and led growth or led marketing teams.
And, you know, oftentimes we found most agencies we worked with have kind of sometimes a limited scope of ownership of a channel or aspects of varying channels.

For example, like, you know, on the SEO side, maybe they help with keyword research, you know, on-page optimization ideas, maybe implementation of that technical SEO, but they may not provide the content, maybe the content strategy, but not writing the content or it's extra.

But for us, you know, as I was mentioning earlier, we want to really own that end to end of setting that right strategy, starting to collaborate with you on like what the right outputs of that look like and how to create amazing pieces of content, creating that content for you, creating the necessary things like imagery to support those user experiences.
And then for the vast majority of our accounts, there may only be like one or two that we don't do this for, we stage and publish the content as well.
That just enables us to move super, super fast.
If we can move fast, we can drive results all the much faster for you.

In terms of where we apply these workflows and essentially like where this content can live, what types of content, et cetera, you know, we've had no shortage of different things we've tested and ideas of where we can take these AI-driven workflows and do some really cool things.
At the end of the day, what we've found after a lot of experimentation, is
Where we work best and who are the best fits for us is those where, excuse me, that's at this editorial content layer.
And what that means to us is longer form content that can, that could mean blogs, but not necessarily limited to blogs.
There's oftentimes different applications of longer form content that can live on your website.

But the purpose of why we'd like to start here is that just provides us a lot of, just to lay that right foundation from a strategic perspective to collaborate or calibrate with you on things like voice, tone, structure, the right topics we should be writing about.
Are those topics resonating with the audience we care about?
What kind of signals are we getting back on their engagement?
How does that inform what we do next?
From there, that naturally should open paths for different forms of distribution of how we can start to think about where this.
Content can be leveraged in multiple places, where it sits alongside email, how we introduce automation to that, where it social fit into the mix, et cetera.
On the conversion front, you were mentioning lead magnets, like leveraging what we do in creation of content and imagery, et cetera.
We can spin up lead magnets quite quickly, as opposed to how I've at least had to do this in the past.
You know, it was not too unusual.
A lot of times when I would do this internally, or sometimes those lead magnets would take like a quarter, sometimes less.

But usually in that ballpark, where for us, like when we have the right, you know, strategy set and have the right game plan, like that's something we can turn around quite quickly in the matter of like, you know, a week or two, as opposed to a quarter.
But the point being is like, this isn't, those aren't places that we typically would start our engagements and do as just a core offering that we would, you know, only offer that.

And so for us, we're very rare we'll go below 12 months.
Occasionally, if we feel strongly about the partnership, we'll consider it.

can be flexible on a number of pieces of content that's delivered from that and then a 12-month agreement from there.

What we have found is what enables us to put out high quality content repeatedly rather than some of the more

And so the key here is, how can we ensure we are really mindful of the jobs to be done, of your personas, the key questions they have associated to those jobs, and how can we have very rich, amazing answers to that?

essentially have a set of different coding agents that enables us to spin up and customize different workflows, and, you know, really a whole variety of different steps that enable us to, you know, do things like, how do we, how do we...

Got it.

But not our, like, our core offering really is to be that end-to-end service offering that helps enable teams to build that right strategy, scale that right content, essentially.
So who would be our main contact at GrowthX?
Yeah, typical interaction would be with the managing directors and managing editors.
And so, you know, on the strategy side, they're both involved, but typically that lives in, at the end of the day, is owned by the managing director.

The managing editor is heavily involved in, again, just, like, the production of, like, consistently high-quality content, editing the content, hearing the feedback from you on, like, is this landing, is it not?
Okay, how do we take that feedback and adapt that into what we do moving forward?
And so making sure we're essentially coordinating on the right topics and what's coming.

I go to Bruno and say, hey, Bruno, I want a white paper.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introduce GrowthX's AI-driven content creation services to Seel's marketing team for potential partnership.](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Seel is at the very beginning of their content marketing journey, with no existing strategy or content](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=66.0)
  - [GrowthX offers end-to-end AI-powered content creation with human oversight, from strategy to publishing](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=97.0)
  - [Typical engagement: 6-8 week strategy sprint ($20K), followed by 12-month contract ($9-15K/month)](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=1112.0)
  - [GrowthX's approach combines AI workflows with high-caliber editorial team for quality assurance](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=898.0)

## Topics

### Seel's Current Marketing Situation

  - [Currently at "minus one" in terms of content strategy and creation](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=123.0)
  - [8-figure ARR achieved through sales-led, direct-to-customer approach](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=183.0)
  - [Minimal inbound leads, primarily through Shopify presence](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=141.0)
  - [Urgency driven by realistic revenue growth targets and need for content to support growth tactics](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=167.0)

### GrowthX's Content Creation Approach

  - [AI-driven workflows with human-in-the-loop components](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=936.0)
  - [End-to-end service: strategy, creation, imagery, staging, and publishing](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=280.0)
  - [Focus on editorial content layer (e.g., blogs, longer-form website content)](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=668.0)
  - [Can create lead magnets (e.g., white papers) in 1-2 weeks, including design](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=735.0)
  - [Team led by former TechCrunch editor-in-chief, supported by experienced managing directors and editors](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=852.0)

### GrowthX's Technology and Process

  - [Multiple AI workflows for research, brief development, and content creation](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=936.0)
  - [Combines public information with client-provided context](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=995.0)
  - [6-8 week strategy sprint to develop deep customer insights and content roadmap](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=1009.0)
  - [Ongoing collaboration via Slack, Notion, and Airtable for content coordination](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=1418.0)

### Engagement Structure and Pricing

  - [Typical engagement: 2-month strategy sprint followed by 12-month agreement](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=1112.0)
  - [Strategy sprint starts at $20K](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=1567.0)
  - [Ongoing monthly fee ranges from $9K to $15K, depending on needs](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=1572.0)
  - [Primary contacts are managing directors and managing editors](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=1432.0)

## Next Steps

  - [GrowthX to send SOW (Statement of Work) and sales deck to Seel team](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=1517.0)
  - [Seel to review materials and socialize with C-level executives for buy-in](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=1524.0)
  - [Potential follow-up call to be scheduled after internal discussions](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=1532.0)
  - [Seel to provide information on key competitors for GrowthX to analyze opportunities](https://fathom.video/share/RBhyszQHYW4yx2zpDKL3bvEyznNXVyJy?tab=summary&timestamp=1487.0)
